div1.entryFree: Chant, vb., to sing: the free maids do use to c. it, Tw. II, 4, 47. the lark that tirra lirra --s, Wint. IV, 3, 9. the birds c. melody on every bush, Tit. II, 3, 12. she --ed snatches of old tunes, Hml. IV, 7, 178. Followed by a superfluous it: she hears them (his hounds and horn) c. it lustily, Ven. 869. -- Having to before the thing addressed or celebrated in song: --ing faint hymns to the cold fruitless moon, Mids. I, 1, 73. he so --s to the sleeve-hand, Wint. IV, 4, 211. he --s a doleful hymn to his own death, John V, 7, 22.
